LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS FASTER LIGHTER BETTER, INC, DBA COPPER.IS & SCOTT FRYXELL'S MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING ARBITRATION AND STAYING ACTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS NOT WITHIN THE SCOPE OF THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT TO ARBITRATE. IT ALLEGES THE VIOLATION OF A DIFFERENT (AND ORAL) AGREEMENT BETWEEN THE PARTIES. THE WRITTEN CONSULTING AGREEMENT DOES NOT HAVE AN INTEGRATION CLAUSE OR PURPORT TO SUPERCEDE ANY EARLIER AGREEMENTS. AS A SEPARATE AND ALTERNATIVE GROUND TO DENY THE MOTION, DEFENDANTS WAIVED THEIR RIGHT TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. THE COMPLAINT WAS FILED ON JULY 19, 2012. THERE IS NO AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSE DEMANDING ARBITRATION. TRIAL IS SET FOR JULY 15, 2013. THIS MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION WAS FILED JUNE 5, 2013, AND WAS NOTICED FOR HEARING A LITTLE MORE THAN 2 WEEKS BEFORE TRIAL. FURTHER TO THE PREJUDICE OF PLAINTIFF, DEFENDANTS HAVE TAKEN ADVANTAGE OF JUDICIAL DISCOVERY PROCEDURES NOT AVAILABLE IN ARBITRATION. SEE SAINT AGNES MED. CTR. V. PACIFICARE OF CALIF. (2003) 31 CAL.4TH 1187, 1203-1204. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

REPLY TO OPPOS TO PLTF'S MTN TO REOPEN DISCO; POS FILED BY PLAINTIFF BELLINGRATH, THOMPSON
MINI MINUTES FOR MAR-11-2014 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

DISCOVERY 302, THE TENTATIVE RULING REGARDING PLAINTIFF THOMPSON BELLINGRATH'S MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY ON A LIMITED BASIS IS ADOPTED AS FOLLOWS: THIS IS AN ACTION TO RECOVER COMPENSATION DUE TO PLAINTIFF FOR INTER ALIA SERVICES RENDERED PURSUANT TO AN ORAL AGREEMENT THAT WAS APPARENTLY CONFIRMED BY EMAIL FROM DEFENDANT. THE DISCOVERY MOTION AT ISSUE HERE IS ONE BY PLAINTIFF TO REOPEN DISCOVERY ON A LIMITED BASIS TO OBTAIN COPIES OF 6 "CANCELLED" (IT IS NOT CLEAR WHY THEY WERE CANCELLED) CHECKS WRITTEN BY DEFENDANT TO "ME [PLAINTIFF] AND SCOTT [WHOSE IDENTITY IS NOT SPECIFIED BUT APPARENTLY IS PLAINTIFF'S BUSINESS PARTNER] IN THE AMOUNT OF $6000.00 EACH, DATED FROM JANUARY 2012 TO MARCH 2012, WHICH DISCOVERY REQUEST, WHILE ARGUABLY TARDY AND TIME BARRED, AS CONTENDED BY DEFENDANT, WOULD, PLAINTIFF CONTENDS, ASSIST HIM IN ESTABLISHING HIS CLAIM FOR COMPENSATION UNDER THE ORAL AGREEMENT. THROUGHOUT THE COURSE OF HIS ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT PLAINTIFF HAS ALTERNATIVELY APPEARED PRO PER AND BY AT LEAST 2 OTHER COUNSEL. DURING THIS TIME PERIOD DEFENDANT HAS ALSO PURSUED A MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION OF PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM, WHICH MOTION WAS ULTIMATELY DENIED ON APPEAL. IF THERE HAS BEEN SOME POSSIBLE TARDINESS IN PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL LIMITED DISCOVERY HERE, THAT MAY BE EXPLAINED AT LEAST IN PART BY THE SOMEWHAT CONVOLUTED PROCEDURAL HISTORY HERE. PLAINTIFF'S DISCOVERY REQUEST HERE SEEMS OBJECTIVELY TO BE BOTH VERY LIMITED, JUSTIFIED, AND CLEARLY DIRECTED TOWARD EVIDENCE (THE CHECKS) THAT APPEAR TO BE USEFUL IN MAKING HIS CASE. THEREFORE, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION IS GRANTED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE PRO TEM: ADRIAN A. COLLEY; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT -

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Reopen Discovery On A Limited Basis
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF THOMPSON BELLINGRATH's Motion To Reopen Discovery On A Limited Basis is to be heard at 9 am on the Discovery calendar. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

Plaintiff Thompson Bellingrath'S Motion To Reopen Discovery On A Limited Basis
Off Calendar Mar-11-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Matthew D. Metzger, Esq., appearing for the Plaintiff;
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

Matter called. No argument held. The Court and Counsel confer regarding the proposed order and the date for plaintiff to file the motion to compel. The tentative ruling regarding Plaintiff Thompson Bellingrath's Motion To Reopen Discovery On A Limited Basis is adopted as follows: This is an action to recover compensation due to plaintiff for inter alia services rendered pursuant to an oral agreement that was apparently confirmed by email from defendant. The discovery motion at issue here is one by plaintiff to reopen discovery on a limited basis to obtain copies of 6 "cancelled" (it is not clear why they were cancelled) checks written by defendant to "me [plaintiff] and Scott [whose identity is not specified but apparently is plaintiff's business partner] in the amount of $6000.00 each, dated from January 2012 to March 2012, which discovery request, while arguably tardy and time barred, as contended by defendant, would, plaintiff contends, assist him in establishing his claim for compensation under the oral agreement. Throughout the course of his action against defendant plaintiff has alternatively appeared pro per and by at least 2 other counsel. During this time period defendant has also pursued a motion to compel arbitration of plaintiff's claim, which motion was ultimately denied on appeal. If there has been some possible tardiness in plaintiff's motion to compel limited discovery here, that may be explained at least in part by the somewhat convoluted procedural history here. Plaintiff's discovery request here seems objectively to be both very limited, justified, and clearly directed toward evidence (the checks) that appear to be useful in making his case. Therefore, plaintiff's motion is granted. Order signed. Judge Pro Tem: Adrian A. Colley; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Not Reported. =302/JPT

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Arb Adn Staying Action
Off Calendar Jun-28-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - THOMPSON BELLINGRATH, TEL. NO. (212) 327-0000, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. LEE HEPNER OF GONZALEZ & LEIGH LLP, TEL. NO. 912-5920 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS FASTER LIGHTER BETTER, INC, DBA COPPER.IS & SCOTT FRYXELL's Motion For Order Compelling Arbitration and Staying Action is denied. Plaintiff's complaint is not within the scope of the parties' agreement to arbitrate. It alleges the violation of a different (and oral) agreement between the parties. The written consulting agreement does not have an integration clause or purport to supercede any earlier agreements. As a separate and alternative ground to deny the motion, Defendants waived their right to compel arbitration. The Complaint was filed on July 19, 2012. There is no affirmative defense demanding arbitration. Trial is set for July 15, 2013. This motion to compel arbitration was filed June 5, 2013, and was noticed for hearing a little more than 2 weeks before trial. Further to the prejudice of Plaintiff, Defendants have taken advantage of judicial discovery procedures not available in arbitration. See Saint Agnes Med. Ctr. v. PacifiCare of Calif. (2003) 31 Cal.4th 1187, 1203-1204. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)
